model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: This is one of the closest matchups in the bracket, with Santa Clara actually ranking higher in Torvik (#28 vs #32) thanks to an elite offense ranked 15th nationally (AdjO 125.0), while Kentucky holds the KenPom edge (#28 vs #35) with a significantly better defense (AdjD 99.1 vs 104.3). The razor-thin 2.5-point spread reflects the near coin-flip nature of this game, but Kentucky's SEC schedule—despite their disappointing 21-13 record—provides battle-testing that Santa Clara's WCC slate cannot replicate, and the Wildcats' roughly 5-point defensive efficiency advantage should be the deciding factor against a Broncos team whose 76th-ranked defense may struggle to get stops in a high-pressure tournament environment. Kentucky's physical, defensively-oriented basketball tends to travel better in March than Santa Clara's offense-first approach built against weaker competition, giving the Wildcats a narrow but real edge.

reasoningStep.title: Kentucky vs Santa Clara – Midwest 7/10 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: A near coin-flip matchup where Kentucky's defensive superiority and SEC schedule strength narrowly outweigh Santa Clara's elite offense and higher Torvik ranking, with the 2.5-point spread confirming this as one of the tightest games in the bracket.
evidence: Kentucky KenPom #28 (AdjEM +21.47, AdjO 120.5, AdjD 99.1) vs Santa Clara KenPom #35 (AdjEM +19.42, AdjO 123.7, AdjD 104.3)
Santa Clara Torvik #28 (AdjOE 125.0 rank 15th, AdjDE 103.7 rank 76th) vs Kentucky Torvik #32 (AdjOE 119.9 rank 40th, AdjDE 99.9 rank 32nd)
Kentucky 21-13 in the SEC vs Santa Clara 26-8 in the WCC; Kentucky's schedule strength significantly higher
Spread: Kentucky -2.5, moneyline Kentucky -150 / Santa Clara +125, O/U 160.5
Santa Clara reached WCC Tournament championship game, losing to Gonzaga 79-68; Kentucky beat Missouri 78-72 in SEC Tournament
Kentucky's ~5-point defensive efficiency advantage (AdjD 99.1 vs 104.3) offsets Santa Clara's ~4-point offensive efficiency advantage (AdjO 123.7 vs 120.5)

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
